titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Unitarian Universalist Women's Heritage Society Records
Unitarian Universalist Women's Heritage Society Records
titlestmt.author: Divinity Library staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 13, 2020 at 23:20

did.unittitle: Unitarian Universalist Women's Heritage Society Records
did.abstract: This collection contains biographical and historical information about Unitarian Universalist women and churches.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Subject File, II. Oral History Transcripts, III. Pamphlets and Books.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Unitarian Universalist Women's Heritage Society (UUHW) and the Unitarian Universalist Historical Society (UUHS) joined to form the Unitarian Universalist History and Heritage Society (UUHHS) in July 2011.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: From the Andover Newton Theological School Collection, transferred to the Yale Divinity School Library in 2017.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Unitarian Universalist Women's Heritage Society Records (RG 301), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains a subject file of biographical and historical material related to various individuals and topics as well as oral history transcripts and publications documenting the work of Unitarian Universalists, particularly women.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "An appeal in favor of that class of Americans called Africans"

did.unittitle: First American Congress of Liberal Religious Societies
New England Hymns - Singing, Shouting, Celebrating
Salt Lake City, Utah: First Unitarian Church - Herstories
Women of Courage: Games, Puzzles and Stories for Kids
Women's International League for Peace and Freedom
World Journal: Vol 6, No. 2; Vol. 8, No. 1

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The original finding aid has an entry for "Occasional Papers" and the note that "All are in online catalog and shelved with the pamphlet collection."

did.unittitle: "By Their Deeds - being the story of some of our Universalist projects" by Harriet G. Yates
"By Their Works - Biographical Sketches of Universalist Women" by Laura S. Hersey
"Seventy-Five Years .. Onward : A history of the work and progress of Universalist Women between the years 1869 and 1945 "
"Transforming Thought: Feminist Theology in the Context of Unitarian Universalist Women" : Volume II In Honor of Women Working Together - Betty B. Hoskins, Editor
"Unitarian Universalist Women's Heritage Trail: Boston" by Christine Jaronski
Minutes of Woman's Universalist Missionary Society of Massachusetts
Pamphlets related to specific churches in Massachusetts
